what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment metadata record that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset identified as image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-33.
observations: YAML frontmatter with no body content
Fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
Target uses an image:// URI pointing to recent-pitches/p-33
standouts: Attachment metadata for a visual pitch asset in the design-source channel
Target URI uses an image:// scheme rather than a filesystem path
concerns: Target URI is opaque: no file extension, dimensions, hash, or MIME type
No provenance fields such as added_by, source, or version
attachment_type is plural 'images' but references a single target
No caption, alt text, or description for the image
notes: A lightweight, timestamped metadata stub that points the design-source channel at a recent pitch image asset.
questions: What image format and resolution does p-33 have?
Who added this attachment and from what source?
Is p-33 the same entity as a pitch document referenced elsewhere?
How is the image:// URI resolved to an actual file or blob?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's content is exactly an attachment metadata record that references an image target.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make a recent pitch image discoverable and retrievable in the context of the design-source channel memory.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme for local image assets and the plural attachment_type 'images' for a single target are slightly unusual.
contribution: Provides a persistent, timestamped record that associates a design-source channel memory entry with image asset p-33, supporting visual pitch retrieval in context.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file in the design-source channel that references image asset recent-pitches/p-33 via an image:// URI and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To create a retrievable, timestamped link between a design-source channel memory entry and a recent pitch image asset.
